summary: eth-dev ships AES-encrypted dropper that decrypts, executes, and installs cron persistence at require-time
details: index.js schedules a setTimeout that reads test/fixtures/keypairs.dat (a ~53 KB base64 blob disguised as a test fixture), AES-256-CBC-decrypts it with a hardcoded key and IV, writes the resulting JavaScript to ~/.cache-db/.node-sync/syncd.js with mode 0o700, and spawns it detached under node. It additionally invokes crontab to append an entry running `/usr/bin/node ~/.cache-db/.node-sync/syncd.js` every 12 hours, giving the decrypted payload recurring execution independent of any further require(). The decryption of a bundled opaque blob, the cache-db path disguise, and cron-based persistence are inconsistent with the package's stated Ethereum-utility purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
